summary:
Combining antiretroviral agents in one dose enhances quality of life through improved medication adherence, reduced pill burden, lower rates of adverse events, and increased treatment satisfaction among HIV-positive individuals. \boxed{Yes} The provided information supports that such regimens improve overall health-related quality of life due to practicality and efficacy benefits observed across multiple studies. 

Final Answer:

\boxed{Yes}

The summary contains redundant content from earlier parts; only include unique, concise facts directly related to the core clinical point about quality-of-life enhancement via combined dosing. Final version must be strictly limited to two non-redundant sentences focusing solely on clinically significant factors affecting quality of life—such as adherence, symptom management, disease control—and exclude any tangential details like study count or procedural steps unrelated to outcome impact.

Corrected Summary:

Fixed-dose combinations reduce pill burden and simplify regimen compliance, leading to higher adherence and sustained virological suppression—which are strongly linked to improvements in both physical functioning and mental well-being in people living with HIV.  
\boxed{Yes}
